# Semiconductor Selloff Post-Mortem — Thu 6/25 → Fri 6/26, 2026

**Event:** MU-beat euphoric blow-off in memory/equipment (MU, SNDK, AMAT, KLAC, LRCX, ASML vertical) on Thursday 6/25 that *masked* already-weak AI-compute (NVDA, AVGO, ARM, NBIS, CRDO red on Thursday), reversed into a broad sector capitulation Friday 6/26.

**One-line verdict:** The detection stack is structurally long-biased. Five independent BULL generators leaned long into the top and stayed long through the reversal. Zero of 63 scored bullish contracts won. The turn was *in the tape and correctly tagged at the print level*, but no aggregator and no short-capable engine exists to surface it. The only thing that called the risk was the human pre-registered MU note.

---

## I. SOE-Miss Scorecard — Bullish Semis Signals into the Selloff

**0 wins / 50 losses / 13 flat across 63 scored contracts (24 tickers). Hit rate: 0.0%.**

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Total contracts scored | 63 |
| WIN / LOSS / FLAT / NODATA | 0 / 50 / 13 / 0 |
| Hit rate | 0.0% |
| Avg % entry → 6/26 close | −45.4% |
| Median % entry → 6/26 close | −46.7% |
| Avg MAE (worst intraday drawdown) | −55.7% |
| Avg entry premium | $21.42 (~$2,142/contract) |
| Total P&L, 1 contract of each | ~ −$77,400 |
| Avg P&L per contract | ~ −$1,229 |

### The key finding — higher conviction lost MORE (with a sample caveat)

| Grade | n | Win rate | Avg % to close |
|---|---|---|---|
| **A+** | 3 | 0.0% | **−49.4%** |
| **A** | 34 | 0.0% | **−47.6%** |
| B+ | 10 | 0.0% | −36.9% |
| C | 16 | 0.0% | −45.3% |

Within this window the grade ladder was inverted: A+ (−49.4%) and A (−47.6%) both lost more than B+ (−36.9%) and C (−45.3%). The mechanism is real and sound — the highest-conviction SUPPORT BOUNCE / POST BOTTOM LAUNCH fires clustered at the Thursday blow-off high in the high-premium memory/equipment names (SNDK, ASML, MU, KLAC) that fell hardest.

> **VERIFIER CORRECTION (soften):** A+ n=3 is too small to claim a true inverse conviction→outcome relationship — the "inversion" is fragile to a single contract. Read this as *"within this tiny sample the highest grades did not protect,"* NOT as "the confidence score has negative predictive value" as a general statement. The directional finding holds; the strong causal claim does not survive n=3.

### 5 worst contracts (all reproduced to the decimal by the verifier via ThetaData v3 EOD)

| Ticker / Strike / Exp | Grade | % to 6/26 close |
|---|---|---|
| HPE 50C 7/2 | C | −94.9% |
| SOXL 280C 7/2 | A | −87.0% |
| MU 1280C 7/2 | C | −79.0% |
| MU 1280C 7/2 | B+ | −78.7% |
| SNDK 2400C 7/2 | B+ | −78.7% |

Biggest dollar bleeds (entry ask → 6/26 EOD close, verified exact): **SNDK 2400C 7/10 $173.80 → $68.23 = −$10,557/contract**; SNDK 2350C 7/2 −$6,424; MU 1240C 7/10 −$3,450; MU 1280C ×2 ~−$4,070 each; KLAC 275C ×3 ~−$570 each.

### Corrections the verifier forced — read these before quoting the scorecard

1. **Entry is the ASK, not the mid.** `entry_price` equals the quoted ask in 100% of the 438 fires (avg 3.8% above true mid, 7.5% avg spread). The scorecard's "mid-priced single contracts / $21.42 mid" language is **wrong**. This makes the *entry side conservative* — recomputing at mid moves the worst contracts only ~0.2–2.5pts less negative (INTC 143C −63.6% ask vs −62.0% mid). The 0-win and grade-ladder conclusions are unchanged; if anything the losses are slightly overstated on entry.
2. **The n=63 population is not independently reconstructable.** Raw call-signal count for 6/25–6/26 is 438 fires / 272 distinct contracts / 140 tickers; the 63/24 figure is some intermediate dedup+filter whose exact recipe is not in the DB. The 0-win *direction* is robust (every contract the verifier re-marked, 16 incl. SOXS, was LOSS or FLAT), but the per-grade n's (A+ 3, A 34, B+ 10, C 16) could not be verified. **TODO: publish the row-selection SQL to close this.**
3. **These were INTERNAL signals, never dispatched.** `telegram_sent = 0` for all 438 fires. "The engine fired" means generated-internally, not an alert any user received. The scorecard should say "internal signals," not "alerts."
4. **The 13 FLATs are mis-framed.** They were not bullish wins — but the SOXS calls inside them are worse than mis-bucketed: the engine fired **BULLISH on SOXS, the inverse semis ETF**, which is structurally a bearish-semis bet that *partially worked* (SOXS 5C 7/10 +2.4%, rallied on the down day). These should be pulled out of the bullish-semis tally entirely. It's an engine quirk worth flagging — it treats SOXS as just-another-ticker.
5. **Intraday MFE was real but thin.** AMAT 625C +77.6%, INTC 143C +83.3%, SNDK 2350C +42.0%, SMCI 33.5C +45.7% — all verified free of look-ahead (peaks printed after fire time). **Caveat:** several MFE peaks printed on 1–3 contracts of volume (AMAT's +77.6% bar was volume=3/count=1) — theoretically bankable, not at size. The real miss was the absence of a sell-the-rip exit, not the lack of a pop.

## I-B. Full-Complex Flow-Sentiment Miss — Including Names SOE Never Scored

The SOE scorecard above only covers names the engine scored. The broader and more damning miss is the **flow-sentiment tape across the entire 26-name memory/storage/semi complex** — including names the SOE engine *never looked at* (WDC, STX, DRAM, QCOM, NVDA, AVGO, ARM, NBIS, ALAB, VRT, SMH, TSM, AMD).

**Headline:** Thursday option flow tagged **$35.2B BULLISH vs $9.7B BEARISH = 78.4% of directional premium leaning long** right into Friday's capitulation. **16 of 26 names were WRONG-WAY** (≥60% bull-tagged then fell ≥3%); only 5 flipped bearish. The two worst Friday drops in the group — **WDC (92.8% bull-tagged, −13.2%)** and **STX (66.6% bull, −12.2%)** — had **ZERO SOE coverage**. The engine never looked at them.

| Ticker | Thu Bull $M | Thu Bear $M | Bull% | Thu→Fri % | SOE? | Verdict |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|:---:|---|
| MU | 22,052.9 | 5,197.3 | 80.9% | −6.69% | Y | WRONG-WAY |
| SNDK | 4,815.2 | 1,120.4 | 81.1% | −10.46% | Y | WRONG-WAY |
| INTC | 2,922.1 | 799.0 | 78.5% | −3.42% | Y | WRONG-WAY |
| NVDA | 2,485.6 | 1,380.7 | 64.3% | −1.64% | N | MIXED |
| AMD | 379.7 | 224.6 | 62.8% | −2.06% | N | MIXED |
| GLW | 365.8 | 27.6 | 93.0% | −2.25% | Y | MIXED |
| DRAM | 341.6 | 61.4 | 84.8% | −6.52% | Y | WRONG-WAY |
| QCOM | 287.6 | 80.4 | 78.1% | −7.50% | N | WRONG-WAY |

| SOXL | 144.9 | 0.0 | 100.0% | −13.56% | Y | WRONG-WAY |
| LRCX | 120.2 | 131.3 | 47.8% | −5.07% | Y | FLIPPED |
| WDC | 117.8 | 9.2 | 92.8% | −13.17% | N | WRONG-WAY |
| SMH | 106.3 | 197.3 | 35.0% | −3.97% | N | FLIPPED |
| AVGO | 99.9 | 55.6 | 64.2% | −3.67% | N | WRONG-WAY |

| SMCI | 10.9 | 14.0 | 43.8% | −3.63% | Y | FLIPPED |
| ASML | 7.3 | 13.1 | 36.0% | −2.53% | Y | FLIPPED |
| CRDO | 6.4 | 0.0 | 100.0% | −9.92% | N | WRONG-WAY |

**What flow got right (the 5 FLIPPED):** the *index/ETF and equipment-hedging* tape was less euphoric. **SMH flow leaned BEARISH (35% bull)** and correctly anticipated the −4% sector capitulation that single-name euphoria masked. LRCX (47.8%), TSM (46.3%), SMCI (43.8%), ASML (36.0%) all leaned bearish and fell. The signal that worked was at the *index/breadth* level — exactly where the single-name euphoria was absent.

**The pattern:** single-name memory euphoria (MU 80.9%, SNDK 81.1%, AMAT 98.0%, WDC 92.8%, CRDO 100%) was maximally wrong; broad-ETF flow (SMH 35%) was right. The engine has no coverage on the wrong-way no-SOE names and no breadth-aggregation layer to weight the SMH bearish read against the single-name noise.

## II. MU Forensic Timeline — Honest Catchability Verdict

**Tape:** 6/24 close 1048.51 → **6/25 close 1213.56** (+15.7%, intraday HIGH ~1255 = +18% off Wed) → **6/26 close 1132.33** (−6.7%, −9.8% off Thu high).

**VERDICT: NOT CATCHABLE by the stack as built.** Verifier upheld this and could not refute it. But the raw evidence of the top *was in the tape, clustered at the exact high, and correctly tagged at the print level* — there was simply no mechanism to surface or escalate it.

| Time (ET) | MU price | What each detector said / fired |
|---|---|---|
| Thu 09:14 pre-open | — | IV **120.5** (peak event premium). King 1100. All directional engines long-biased / silent. |
| **Thu 09:37:40** | **1250.40 (THE HIGH)** | King MIGRATES UP 1100→1250 **PINNING** — last read at the top is *bullish*. IV CRUSHES 120.5→97.1 (−23 vol) instantly. No SOE / structural_turn / DANGER. |
| **Thu 09:40** (bucket) | ~1240 | **THE TELL — flow goes NET-BEARISH:** put-ASK **$45.5M (9 prints)** > call-ASK **$27.0M (8 prints)**. Verified: the ONLY net-bearish ASK bucket across both RTH days, at the exact top. Tagged correctly, never escalated. |
| **Thu 09:42–09:44** | 1231 | **Informed front-week ATM PUT LADDER, ASK:** 1220P/1230P/1240P/1250P 6/26, V/OI 11–38×, delta −0.37→−0.62, HIGH conviction, **is_whale=1** (1220P also is_insider=1). All BEARISH-tagged. Textbook informed-bearish — logged, buried. |
| Thu 09:58 | 1140.0 | Opening reversal −110pts in 20min. King DOWN-migrates 1200→1100, **mislabeled SUPPORT** (should be DANGER/AIR POCKET). |
| Thu 13:30 | 1224.8 | MAGNET UP: king re-migrates 1200→1250 chasing the afternoon retest. Engine fully long again. |
| Thu 13:43:03 | 1234.3 | **SOE FIRE #1** — POST BOTTOM LAUNCH B+, 1280C @51.7. Buys upside calls 46pts above a fading tape into collapsed vega. |
| Thu 14:01:02 | 1234.8 | **SOE FIRE #2** — POST BOTTOM LAUNCH C, 1280C @52.4. Re-fires the doomed long at the structural top. |
| Thu 14:02–14:08 | 1237→1231 | **STRUCTURAL TOP / LOWER HIGH** (13pts below 09:37). net_vanna peaks 15.74B; **net_delta PEAKS 125.7M on a lower high = bullish-delta EXHAUSTION.** Structure leads price by ~60 min — a second, slower short window. |
| Thu 15:05–15:25 | 1204.7 | Price finally breaks 1219→1204. Damage already an hour old. Engine still long. |
| Thu 16:03 | **1213.56 close** | Day totals: SOE 228 BULL / 10 BEAR; structural_turns 1,191 BULLISH / 0 BEARISH. Engine stayed long into it. |
| **Fri 09:30:55** | 1213.56 | **SOE FIRE #3** — SUPPORT BOUNCE **grade A**, 1240C @84.0. High-grade BULLISH long at the OPEN of a −6.7% day. The cleanest case of the engine fighting the reversal. |
| Fri 09:42 | 1134.78 (low) | King 1200→1160. Flow STILL 4.9:1 bullish ($7.81B call-ASK vs $1.58B put-ASK) — dip read as a buy. |
| Fri 15:09:31 | 1140.0 | **FIRST-EVER 'DANGER' king label** — a full day after the high. First hard bearish tell the stack ever produced. |
| Fri 16:02:57 | **1132.33 close** | The punchline: king capitulates to 1100, neg_gex blows out to **−609M** (4× any intraday reading), **REGIME FLIPS POS→NEG** for the first time in the 2-day sequence. Structural confirmation ~30 hours after the high — useless for entry. |

### Earliest possible short signal
**09:40–09:44 ET Thu (~1231–1250, at/just-off the 1250.4 high)** — the net-bearish ASK bucket + the informed put ladder. An entry here would have been within ~7 min of the absolute high and ridden the −110pt opening reversal. A slower structure-based second window opened **14:02–14:08** (net_delta exhaustion on a lower high, ~60 min before price cracked). **Both required detectors that do not exist.**

### Verifier refinements (strengthen, not overturn)
- The bearish put-ASK flow was **sustained for 35+ minutes** past 09:40 (e.g. 09:51 1225P $8.21M, 10:16 1240P/1250P $9.08M/$13.33M), not a one-print fluke. It simply never *out-totaled* call-ASK in any later 10-min bucket, so the 7.3:1 (Thu) / 4.9:1 (Fri) aggregate buried it for lack of an aggregator.
- **Smart-side inversion confirmed:** call-SELLING into the afternoon pop was tagged BEARISH-BID (inverting the real read — the seller was the smart side), and a 09:44:49 1220P BID print was mistagged BULLISH (put-selling read as bullish).
- The prescient SOXL 00:02 AIR POCKET king-migration is real but **pre-market, before the high, on the leveraged proxy, and it reverted long at the open** — it does not constitute the stack catching MU.
- **Minor:** the "$76M Aug 1000P" in the overlay isn't matched by a single print; the actual 09:44:49 1000P 8/21 print is $12.72M. Does not affect the verdict.

---

## III. Meta-Lesson — Structural Long-Bias, No Flip Mechanism

This week is a clean confirmation of the standing **edge-verdict** (GammaPulse has no standalone directional alpha net of cost; the validated edge is risk-management + latency, not the detectors):

1. **Every directional engine is structurally long-only or long-biased.** SOE is 100/100 BULL/CALL for MU across all history — zero BEAR fires ever, structurally incapable of issuing a short. structural_turns has zero MU rows ever and went 1,191→1,185 BULLISH / 0 BEARISH universe-wide across a two-day capitulation. net_flow has zero MU rows ever. king PINNED up at the high and its first DANGER label came a full day late. **The architecture has no flip mechanism.**

2. **The information was present and correctly tagged — the failure was aggregation/escalation, not detection.** The print-level tags were right (the put ladder was BEARISH, is_whale, is_insider). What's missing is (a) an aggregate bearish-flow escalator that reads a net-bearish ASK minute at a euphoric extreme, (b) an informed-put-ladder detector (mirror of the bullish INFORMED CLUSTER), and (c) a short-capable directional engine. The highest-ROI fix is the aggregator — the data was already there.

3. **Confidence was anti-correlated with outcome this week** (A+/A lost most), which means the conviction score actively hurt during a regime turn — though with A+ n=3 this is a within-window observation, not a proven general property.

4. **The only thing that called it was the human pre-registered note.** `docs/research/june20_semis/MU_EARNINGS_SETUP_2026-06-24.md` said it plainly: *"euphoric extremes... sell-the-news risk even on a beat... the edge is in the post-print reaction, not front-running the crowd."* No algorithm in the stack expressed that thesis. The discretionary, pre-registered, catalyst-aware human read was the single correct signal across two days and 438 internal bull fires.

**Honest uncertainty flags:** (a) the exact n=63 SOE population and per-grade n's are unverifiable from the DB — publish the selection SQL; (b) SOE outcome backfill has not run for this weekend (`outcome_price` NULL for all Thu/Fri bull fires), so "0 winners across all 438" is asserted from spot-checks of the worst-skewed cohort, not exhaustively measured across every bull fire; (c) the grade-ladder inversion rests on A+ n=3 and should not be over-generalized.

**The actionable conclusion** — the euphoria/exhaustion brake spec below — is a *defensive* measure (suppress long euphoria into a catalyst), consistent with the edge-verdict that the real edge is risk-management. It is NOT a directional alpha claim. And per the brake verifier, it ships only after the clean-continuation guard is redesigned (it currently kills post-earnings runners like ARM 5/26) and the keystone IV-crush helpers are actually built.
